items.title: Homun Operational Workspace
items.outcome: A model-independent workspace where Homun remembers projects, creates real deliverables, uses connected tools and asks before taking sensitive actions.
items.whyNow: Small teams need a useful operational foundation before adding shared processes and specialized workflows.
items.strategicRole: The operational foundation on which Homun Flow and official workflow packs are built.
items.publicUpdate: Available in current Homun desktop releases; shipped improvements remain in the changelog.

items.title: Homun Flow
items.outcome: Turn recurring work into a visible process that people and Homun can complete, review and hand over together.
items.whyNow: Homun already has durable tasks, approvals, tools and agent execution; the next product step is to turn those foundations into understandable team processes.
firstRelease: Processes with explicit activities and dependencies
A lightweight native board derived from process state
Assignment to a person, role or Homun
Artifacts and evidence linked to activities
items.strategicRole: The reusable foundation for every official business workflow developed next.
items.publicUpdate: Durable task, approval and audit foundations are verified; the product process model and visible board come next.

items.title: Team Spaces & Roles
items.outcome: Give every person and Homun the right context, responsibilities and permissions.
items.whyNow: A shared process needs explicit ownership and access boundaries before it can coordinate a real team safely.
firstRelease: Separation of personal and company memory
notIncludedYet: A general-purpose replacement for Slack or Microsoft Teams
items.strategicRole: The identity and responsibility layer for multi-person Homun Flow processes.
items.publicUpdate: The first release is bounded around roles, ownership, review and permissioned shared context.

items.title: Homun Mobile
items.outcome: Keep projects moving, review work and approve sensitive actions wherever you are.
items.whyNow: A team process cannot depend on every decision-maker being at a desktop when reviews and approvals are needed.
firstRelease: Secure pairing with the user's Homun environment
items.strategicRole: The review, approval and capture surface that keeps team processes moving away from the desk.
items.publicUpdate: Mobile is a decided product extension; secure pairing and approval continuity define the first boundary.

items.title: More Ways to Reach Homun
items.outcome: Bring Homun into the communication channels your company already uses.
items.whyNow: Processes become useful when requests, customers and collaborators can enter through familiar channels without duplicating work.
firstRelease: Clear separation between internal and external participants
Shared channel permission and audit rules
notIncludedYet: Channels without a validated workflow need
items.strategicRole: The channel layer that brings external requests and internal coordination into governed processes.
items.publicUpdate: WhatsApp and Telegram are available; email and Slack are the next channel priorities.

items.title: Adaptive Company Intelligence
items.outcome: Homun learns your company's terminology, knowledge, successful examples and review criteria, progressively adapting to how the business works.
items.whyNow: Reliable adaptation must grow from real processes, corrections and evaluations instead of promising model training before useful company data exists.
firstRelease: Company Knowledge connected to real work
notIncludedYet: One-click SLM training without sufficient examples
Replacing RAG or memory with fine-tuning
items.strategicRole: A credible path from company knowledge and measured workflow outcomes to specialized small models.
items.publicUpdate: The research sequence is Company Profile, Company Knowledge, Process Learning, Evaluation and only then a Specialized SLM.

items.title: Voice & Meeting Capture
items.outcome: Bring voice conversations and meeting context into a process only when explicitly enabled.
items.whyNow: Many decisions begin in conversation, but useful capture requires visible consent, privacy controls and a clear process destination.
items.strategicRole: A controlled input layer for decisions and work that currently disappear after meetings.
items.publicUpdate: Privacy boundaries and process linkage come before interface exploration.

items.title: Developer Platform
items.outcome: A future platform for selected partners and developers to build workflows and capabilities on Homun.
items.whyNow: The extension contract should open only after official workflow packs prove what developers need and which safety boundaries work.
items.strategicRole: The later ecosystem multiplier after official vertical products validate the platform contract.
items.publicUpdate: Official workflow packs come first; the developer platform follows their validation and maturation.

items.title: Client Work
items.outcome: From a client request to a researched, reviewed and approved deliverable.
items.whyNow: Service teams already combine messages, research, documents, review and approvals—the strongest fit for a first complete Homun Flow pilot.
notIncludedYet: A delivery commitment before a pilot is selected
items.strategicRole: Recommended first vertical pilot because it exercises Homun's existing research, artifact, connection and approval foundations.
items.targetTeam: Agencies, consultants, professional practices and small service companies.
items.expectedOutput: A reviewed client deliverable with evidence, approval history and follow-up.
items.publicUpdate: Evaluating pilot teams and the minimum repeatable process definition.

items.title: Sales Operations
items.outcome: Research leads, prepare outreach, update the CRM and maintain consistent follow-up.
items.whyNow: Small sales teams lose continuity between research, approval, CRM updates and follow-up across disconnected tools.
items.strategicRole: A repeatable revenue workflow that combines research, review and connected execution.
items.targetTeam: Small B2B sales teams, founders and account managers.
items.expectedOutput: A qualified lead record, approved outreach and a visible follow-up sequence.
items.publicUpdate: Evaluating which CRM and outreach sequence creates the clearest small-team pilot.

items.title: Content & Marketing
items.outcome: Plan, produce, review and publish content while preserving brand rules and approvals.
items.whyNow: Content teams need consistent context and review more than another isolated generation interface.
items.strategicRole: A visible creative workflow where company adaptation and human review can produce measurable quality gains.
items.targetTeam: Small marketing teams, agencies and founder-led companies.
items.expectedOutput: Approved, brand-consistent content with source context and publication history.
items.publicUpdate: Evaluating the smallest useful pack across brand context, review and publication.

items.title: Internal Operations
items.outcome: Turn company procedures and recurring controls into visible, repeatable work.
items.whyNow: Small companies often depend on undocumented routines and manual checks that disappear when ownership changes.
items.strategicRole: A broad operational vertical that turns company procedures into measurable process data.
items.targetTeam: Operations managers, administration teams and small manufacturers or service companies.
likelySystems: ERP or line-of-business systems through governed connections
items.expectedOutput: A completed recurring control with evidence, exceptions and a reviewable report.
items.publicUpdate: Evaluating which recurring procedure provides the strongest pilot evidence.

items.title: Customer Support
items.outcome: Coordinate incoming requests, proposed answers, escalation and human handoff across channels.
items.whyNow: Support combines channel intake, company knowledge, quality review and clear human ownership in one measurable process.
items.strategicRole: A channel-rich workflow that demonstrates controlled company knowledge and human escalation.
items.targetTeam: Small support teams, service desks and customer-facing operations.
likelySystems: Helpdesk and CRM through governed connections
items.expectedOutput: A contextual response with review, escalation and follow-up history.
items.publicUpdate: Evaluating channel intake and escalation boundaries for a small-team pilot.
